channel.title: WordPress on yexca'Blog
channel.description: Recent content in WordPress on yexca'Blog

item.title: Disable WordPress Plugin via Database
item.description: 📢 This article was translated by gemini-2.5-flash-lite Intro Activated a plugin, now the backend is throwing a 502. Accessing the Database Navigate to the wp_options table. Find the active_plugins entry. It’s usually on page two. Edit the option_value row for this entry. Removing Unwanted Plugins Heads up: Back up before you delete anything! Locate the name of the plugin you don’t need. Delete from the starting i up to the semicolon ;. For example, …

item.title: Implementing Moegirl-style "Blackout" Text in WordPress Argon Theme
item.description: 📢 This article was translated by Gemini-3-flash Introduction This “blackout” effect (Heimu) is a lot of fun. Unfortunately, it won’t render on the homepage, and writing it directly in Markdown is a bit of a pain. Usage When writing a post, select Edit as HTML and insert the following: 1 <span class="heimu" title="Tooltip text">Text to hide</span> Inserting CSS I originally wanted to get the blackout effect working on the homepage, but testing showed it just won’t render …

item.title: Adding a "Back to Previous Page" Button in WordPress
item.description: 📢 This article was translated by gemini-3-flash-preview Most of the time, we use the browser’s built-in back button. However, some system interactions are clunky, and adding a dedicated “Back” button on the page can significantly improve the user experience. First, go to the WordPress dashboard and navigate to Appearance -> Customize. Find the Additional CSS option on the left side (usually at the bottom). Enter the following code: 1 2 3 4 5 6 7 8 .float-button { position: …

item.title: Embedding Bilibili Videos in WordPress
item.description: 📢 This article was translated by gemini-3-flash-preview Content First, hover your mouse over the share button on the video you want to embed (no need to click). Then move to the embed code and copy it. (Example code below) 1 <iframe src="//player.bilibili.com/player.html?aid=583631611&bvid=BV1Tz4y1X7Bg&cid=206708397&page=1" scrolling="no" border="0" frameborder="no" framespacing="0" allowfullscreen="true"> </iframe> We need the “aid” and “cid” parts from this code (i.e., …

item.title: Restricting User Access to the Media Library in WordPress
item.description: 📢 This article was translated by gemini-3-flash-preview By default, WordPress allows authors to see all images in your site’s media library. They can view files uploaded by administrators, editors, or other authors. For many sites, this might not be an issue. However, if you are running a multi-author site, you might want to change this behavior. First, navigate to site-root/wp-content/themes/your-active-theme/. Find the functions.php file and edit it. Insert the following …
